mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market, which supports strong consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE is below its 5-year average, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the federal funds rate above a low threshold indicates a tightening monetary policy stance, which typically leads to higher discount rates for equities and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% and stays above for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with robust payroll expansion indicates healthy consumer demand and employment, which jointly drive corporate revenue and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1%
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns with an accuracy above 55% over any 5-year rolling window, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, a sustained increase in industrial production signals a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity, which is highly supportive of corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth > 0.5% after a period of decline
failure_condition: In identified recovery regimes, INDPRO growth does not correlate positively with 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts indicates weakening consumer confidence and investment in real estate, which often precedes broader economic slowdowns and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls below 600.0 (thousands of units)
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of periods following the trigger, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by the Consumer Price Index, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to investor uncertainty and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL 12-month change is above 3.0%, the average 20-day SPX return is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and declining payrolls signals a rapidly deteriorating labor market, which severely impacts consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to significant negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines by 0.2% in 3 months
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns with an accuracy above 55% over any 5-year rolling window, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth indicates a strong overall economy, which provides a favorable environment for corporate profitability and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of periods when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or the average return is not significantly positive.
